canonical_runtime_note: v1.0 docs r4 is STILL the canonical Damage_Modeling runtime for the wind_tornado_wind cell (cell_CHANGELOG current_pin = wind_tornado_wind@model_v1_0__docs_r4, runtime_behavior_changed=false at v1.0). This file maps onto the PROPOSED, NON-PROMOTED v2.0 docs r1 candidate artifact only (canonical_runtime_artifact=false, promotion_status=proposed, lifecycle_state=candidate, review_status=pressure_tested_screening_proxy, package_inclusion_status=not_included, promotion=not_performed). The existing v1.0-resolved value_basis_from_damage_modeling.json in this same folder is UNCHANGED and remains the canonical mapping. This file is an ADDITIVE, PARALLEL preview and must never be treated as a replacement.
source_value_workbook: docs/method/value_basis/solar_wind_value_breakdown.xlsx::Wind_Map (rows 2-19; value ladder is peril-agnostic, SAME as v1.0) -- staged locally as VALUE_CROSSWALK_wind_tornado_wind__model_v2_0__docs_r1.csv
important_rule: This crosswalk normalizes and reconciles denominator/grain only; it is NOT external validation evidence and defines NO pass/fail bands. It is a source-pathway / normalization layer, NOT a calibration harness. Never tune a curve from this file. Failure-unit IDs, value shares, hazard axes, curve form, and damage-state ordinates are copied VERBATIM from the staged v2 proposed source artifacts -- nothing here is invented.
scope_note: This pair (wind_convective_wind) covers onshore wind turbines under CONVECTIVE wind sub-perils: tornado [T] and straight-line/derecho wind [W]. HURRICANE/TC is fenced OUT (quarantined), same as v1.0. UNLIKE v1.0 (one shared curve family with a D50 shift for tornado), v2 PROPOSES two independently governed pathways on a shared cell ('common spine'): straight_line_convective (downburst/microburst/macroburst/gust-front/local-derecho-outflow) and tornado_direct_hit (conditional severity after Hazard resolves turbine intersection). Both remain screening engineering proxies (model_grade=screening_engineering_proxy, all parameters tier T4_placeholder_or_expert_judgment).

two_pathway_architecture.note: ASCII tree copied from the staged v2 README verbatim.
two_pathway_architecture.tree:
wind_tornado_wind
├─ straight_line_convective
│  └─ downburst / microburst / macroburst / gust-front / local derecho outflow
└─ tornado_direct_hit
   └─ conditional severity after Hazard has resolved turbine intersection and local demand

wind_default_basis_usd_per_MW.note: SAME value ladder as v1.0 -- it is peril-agnostic and UNCHANGED by the v2 proposal. Reconciliation: 1090 (turbine equipment) + 239 (withheld direct other) + 294 (support) = 1623 (physical); 1623 + 345 (excluded) = 1968 (installed).

straight_line_convective.extrapolation_policy: flag below 28 mps; flag above 55 mps; withhold above 70 mps

tornado_direct_hit.formula: x = delivered tornado rotor-effective peak horizontal speed
tornado_direct_hit.extrapolation_policy: zero below 25 mps; flag above 80 mps as terminal-saturation extrapolation; reject EF class without a qualified wind proxy

wind_damage_grain.curve_form: ordered_damage_state_lognormal: Q_j(x) = Phi(ln(x/theta_j)/beta_ln); P0 = 1 - Q1; P1 = Q1 - Q2; P2 = Q2 - Q3; P3 = Q3; DR = P1*c1 + P2*c2 + P3*c3
wind_damage_grain.f_kind: material/structural capacity vs speed (NOT geometry/elevation, NOT material-BOM-share)
wind_damage_grain.owns: severity curves only -- EAL / PML / VaR / TVaR are downstream (hazard catalog + financial model), NOT in this cell
wind_damage_grain.mutual_exclusivity_note: v2 damage states are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE ordered states (no independent component summation), UNLIKE v1.0's independent blade/tower/nacelle/foundation sum. Terminal collapse (DS3) makes independent component sums invalid.

failure_units.failure_unit: Rotor/pitch + nacelle/drivetrain/power/yaw + tower -- the ONLY unit with a curve
failure_units.component: BLADE | HUB | PITCH_DRIVE | NACELLE_STRUCTURE | GEARBOX_GENERATOR | POWER_CONVERTER | YAW_SYSTEM | TOWER_SECTION

failure_units.split_note: must split line (collection) vs point (substation) exposure before any damage modeling
must split road/staging/facility assets before any damage modeling

failure_units.numeric_dr: not_an_intrinsic_damage_ratio -- allocate once after damaged units known

equipment_shares.note: These are VALUE-LADDER LABELS for reporting/normalization ONLY. They are NOT DR caps and must never be interpreted as an intrinsic ceiling on the damage ratio -- the DS3 terminal state already reaches cost_ratio=1.0 (100% of the 1,090 USD/kW turbine-equipment denominator), independent of these share labels.

comparability_caveat: v2 uses a DIFFERENT denominator (the 1,090 USD/kW turbine-equipment atom, evaluated with MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE ordered damage states) than v1.0 (which summed 5 independent structural failure units on the 1,623,000 USD/MW physical base). The staged OLD_VS_NEW_COMPARISON file flags EVERY row 'different_denominators_do_not_treat_as_regression_target'. Never treat a v2 DR as a drop-in replacement for, or a regression test against, a v1.0 DR -- the grains are not the same.
comparison_guidance: As in v1.0: a whole-farm event loss (e.g. Flat Ridge 2 $7.25M net) CANNOT be placed on a per-MW failure-unit DR axis without (a) a confirmed MW denominator and (b) the struck-turbine count. Only per-turbine or per-struck-subsystem figures are grain-comparable to the v2 turbine-equipment curve. A $/MW exceeding the 1,090,000 USD/MW turbine-equipment cap is a GRAIN WARNING, not evidence DR>1.

promotion_status.note: This proposal is deliberately absent from the artifact index and portable library v2.5. Consumers must continue to pin and use the v1.0 docs r4 artifact for any canonical calculation.
